messages.content: Freedom Planet au Girls and Naruto Uzumaki react to When Neera Ask Lilac, Carol and Milla about Dating Naruto. Lilac, Carol and Milla: “W-What? No, we’re just… Enemie" | au: Naruto (son of brevon.).

[Scene: Neera approaches Lilac, Carol, and Milla with a mischievous smile on her face.]

Neera: Hey, Lilac, Carol, Milla, I have a question for you!

Lilac: [Raises an eyebrow] What is it, Neera?

Carol: Yeah, spill it!

Milla: Oh, I'm curious now!

Neera: Alright, brace yourselves. Have you three ever thought about dating Naruto Uzumaki?

[The trio's eyes widen in surprise.]

Lilac: W-What? No, we're just... enemies! We've been fighting against his father, Brevon.

Carol: Yeah, we're on opposite sides! It's not like we have any romantic feelings towards him.

Milla: [Giggles nervously] That's right. We might have had some tough battles with him, but that's all it is.

[Just then, Naruto, son of Brevon, enters the scene.]

Naruto: Did I hear my name? Someone talking about me, ladies?

Lilac: It's... not what it seems, Naruto. Neera was just asking us a silly question.

Carol: Yeah, we're definitely not interested in dating the enemy.

Milla: But we can still be friends, right? That's way better!

Naruto: [Laughs] Of course, guys! Friends it is!

[The tension dissipates as Naruto joins the group, accepting their response with a friendly smile.]

Neera: Well, it seems like I've stirred up some interesting dynamics here! Sorry about that, guys.

Lilac: It's alright, Neera. Just try not to create any more awkward situations, okay?

Carol: Yeah, we have enough enemies to deal with as it is.

Milla: [Giggles] Let's focus on defeating evil together instead!

[The group continues their adventures, the encounter adding a touch of humor to their ongoing battles.]

[End of scene.]
Lilac, Carol and Milla parents react to When Neera Ask Lilac, Carol and Milla about Dating Naruto. Lilac, Carol and Milla: “W-What? No, we’re just… Enemie" | au: Naruto (son of brevon.). part 2

[Scene: Lilac, Carol, and Milla return home after the encounter with Neera.]

Lilac's Mother: Welcome back, my brave little dragon! How was your day?

Lilac: It was... eventful, to say the least.

Milla's Father: What happened, girls? Did something interesting occur during your mission?

Carol: Well, Neera approached us with a rather unexpected question.

Lilac's Father: Oh? What did she ask?

Milla: She asked if we ever thought about dating Naruto Uzumaki, Brevon's son.

Lilac's Mother: Dating? Oh my, that's quite surprising!

Milla's Father: [Chuckles] I can see why that caught you off guard.

Carol: Indeed. We were all taken aback and made it clear that we're just enemies, nothing more.

Lilac's Father: That's good to hear. It's important to maintain focus on your mission and not let personal distractions get in the way.

Lilac's Mother: Yes, it's crucial to remember your priorities. Allies can turn into enemies in an instant, after all.

Milla's Father: Absolutely. However, it's also important to cultivate friendships and positive connections, even with those on the other side.

Lilac: Oh, don't worry, Dad. Naruto seemed to understand our stance and was fine with being friends.

Lilac's Father: That's good to hear, my little hero. As long as boundaries are clear, there's no harm in forming genuine connections with others.

Lilac's Mother: Just remember to stay cautious, girls. Your safety comes first.

Carol: We will, Mom. We always have each other's backs.

Milla's Father: That's the spirit, girls! Keep fighting the good fight and stay true to yourselves.

[The parents give their children reassuring smiles, understanding the complexities of their adventures.]

[End of scene.]
Brevon react to When Neera Ask Lilac, Carol and Milla about Dating Naruto. Lilac, Carol and Milla: “W-What? No, we’re just… Enemie" | au: Naruto (son of brevon.). part 3

[Scene: Brevon, the main antagonist, receives a report about the encounter with Neera from one of his minions.]

Minion: Lord Brevon, I bring news from the field. Neera approached Lilac, Carol, and Milla with an intriguing question.

Brevon: [Narrows his eyes] What did she ask them?

Minion: She asked if they had ever considered dating Naruto Uzumaki, your son.

Brevon: [Surprised] Naruto? Ha, that's preposterous! My enemies dating my own flesh and blood?

Minion: It seems like a harmless joke, Lord Brevon. They appeared confused and quickly denied any such intentions.

Brevon: They better have! My son should not be associated with those who seek to impede our plans.

Minion: It seems they clarified their stance, assuring everyone that they only view each other as enemies.

Brevon: Good, good. We must not let personal feelings interfere with our goals. Such distractions only lead to weakness.

Minion: Indeed, Lord Brevon. It is crucial to maintain focus on our mission.

Brevon: Friendship and alliances are meaningless in the face of power. We must continue to exert dominance and crush those who oppose us.

Minion: Your determination is admirable, Lord Brevon.

Brevon: Remember, loyalty above all else. Naruto needs to understand that his allegiance lies with our cause, not with our enemies.

Minion: I will ensure he remains devoted to your cause, Lord Brevon.

[The scene ends with Brevon contemplating the encounter, his thoughts consumed by his ambitions.]

[End of scene.]

Serpentine (A robotic-armed Snake and General of Lord Brevon's Army) react to When Neera Ask Lilac, Carol and Milla about Dating Naruto. Lilac, Carol and Milla: “W-What? No, we’re just… Enemie" | au: Naruto (son of brevon.). part 3.5

[Scene: Serpentine, a robotic-armed snake and general of Lord Brevon's army, overhears the report about the encounter with Neera.]

Serpentine: Dating Naruto? Ha! That's an unexpected twist.

Minion: General Serpentine, it seems Neera posed the question to Lilac, Carol, and Milla, and they denied any romantic interest in Naruto.

Serpentine: Ah, I see. They understand their place as enemies, good.

Minion: Indeed, General. It appeared more like a harmless joke than anything serious.

Serpentine: Jokes or not, we cannot afford to let personal connections weaken our resolve. Our loyalty lies with Lord Brevon and his cause.

Minion: Of course, General. We must eliminate all distractions and focus on achieving victory.

Serpentine: Precisely. Naruto is Lord Brevon's son, and he must remember his duty is to our side. No room for sentimentality or weakness.

Minion: Should we reinforce this message to Naruto, General?

Serpentine: Yes. Remind him of his purpose, his allegiance. Loyalty to family is secondary to loyalty to our mission.

Minion: Understood, General Serpentine. I will make sure Naruto understands his role.

Serpentine: Good. We must remain strong, united, and unwavering. Our enemies seek to dismantle our plans, and we will not allow that.

[The scene fades out with Serpentine immersing himself in thoughts of power and unwavering loyalty.]

[End of scene.]

Magister (A wise Earth Dragon and the Heriditary Ruler of Shang Tu) react to When Neera Ask Lilac, Carol and Milla about Dating Naruto. Lilac, Carol and Milla: “W-What? No, we’re just… Enemie" | au: Naruto (son of brevon.). part 4

[Scene: Magister, a wise Earth Dragon and the hereditary ruler of Shang Tu, is approached with information about the encounter with Neera.]

Magister: Ah, I sense a disturbance. What news do you bring?

Messenger: Magister, there was an unexpected incident involving Neera and our young heroes. She asked if they had considered dating Naruto, Lord Brevon's son.

Magister: Dating? That is quite a surprising question.

Messenger: Indeed, Magister. However, Lilac, Carol, and Milla quickly made it clear that they view each other as enemies, denying any romantic interest.

Magister: I commend their honesty and clarity amidst such a perplexing query. It is vital to remain focused on our shared vision of peace.

Messenger: Yes, Magister. They understand the importance of their mission.

Magister: It is crucial for them to adhere to their principles. Unrequited feelings can give rise to distractions and conflicts.

Messenger: Should we provide guidance to the young heroes, Magister?

Magister: We must remind them of the path they have chosen. Friendship, trust, and unity are essential, but romantic entanglements risk muddling their purpose.

Messenger: I will ensure they receive this counsel, Magister.

Magister: Good. We must protect the fragile harmony we have established and guard against the influence of divided loyalties.

Messenger: Understood, Magister. We will support them in prioritizing their duties and staying true to their calling.

Magister: Their resolve will serve as a beacon for our cause, illuminating the path towards a brighter future.

[The scene concludes with Magister reflecting on the delicate balance between duty and personal connections.]

[End of scene.]

Zao (A mischievous Red Panda and the Mayor of Shang Mu) react to When Neera Ask Lilac, Carol and Milla about Dating Naruto. Lilac, Carol and Milla: “W-What? No, we’re just… Enemie" | au: Naruto (son of brevon.). part 5

[Scene: Zao, a mischievous Red Panda and the Mayor of Shang Mu, overhears the news about Neera's question.]

Zao: Well, well, well, what do we have here? Neera spicing things up, huh?

Assistant: Mayor Zao, it seems Neera asked Lilac, Carol, and Milla if they had ever considered dating Naruto Uzumaki, Lord Brevon's son.

Zao: Oh, Neera always knows how to stir the pot! And what did our brave heroines have to say about it?

Assistant: They denied any romantic interest in Naruto, emphasizing that they are enemies, not potential partners.

Zao: Haha! Good on them for staying focused on the mission. Love and war don't mix well, you know.

Assistant: Indeed, Mayor Zao. It's crucial that they keep their priorities straight.

Zao: But hey, a little bit of unexpected romance could have added some spice to the battles! Imagine the drama!

Assistant: Mayor Zao, their battles are challenging enough as it is. We shouldn't encourage any distractions.

Zao: I know, I know. Duty comes first, blah blah. But a Mayor can't help but fantasize about dramatic plot twists, you know!

Assistant: [Sighs] That may be true, Mayor Zao, but let's not encourage any more complications in their already intense lives.

Zao: Fine, fine, you're right. I'll reign in my mischievous thoughts. But deep down, I can't help but root for a little romantic adventure.

Assistant: Let's hope they find fulfillment in their battles against evil and leave romance for another time, Mayor Zao.

Zao: Agreed, my sensible friend. Now, let's get back to running this city with a mischievous twinkle in our eyes!

[The scene ends with Zao chuckling mischievously, his imagination running wild with romantic possibilities.]

[End of scene.]

Spade (A Member of the Red Scarves, Dail's Half-Brother and Rival to Lilac & Carol) react to When Neera Ask Lilac, Carol and Milla about Dating Naruto. Lilac, Carol and Milla: “W-What? No, we’re just… Enemie" | au: Naruto (son of brevon.). part 6

[Scene: Spade, a member of the Red Scarves and Dail's half-brother, listens in on the conversation about Neera's question.]

Spade: Dating Naruto, huh? That would be quite the twist, wouldn't it?

Assistant: Spade, it appears that Lilac, Carol, and Milla quickly dismissed the idea, clarifying that they view each other as enemies.

Spade: [Raises an eyebrow] Enemies turned potential love interests? That's an intriguing concept. But they made it clear they're not interested, huh?

Assistant: Yes, their loyalty to their mission and their focus on their battles against Lord Brevon remain steadfast.

Spade: Well, I suppose it's better that way. Romance can complicate things, especially when loyalties are divided.

Assistant: That's true, Spade. They must prioritize their goals and the greater good above personal interests.

Spade: I can't deny that it's fascinating to see how their relationships evolve, even if it's just friendship.

Assistant: Indeed, their camaraderie and unity are essential for their success against Lord Brevon and his forces.

Spade: I'll continue to be their rival on the battlefield, but it's fascinating to witness their bonds grow stronger. Love or not.

Assistant: It's good to see that you understand the importance of their unity, even as rivals.

Spade: Of course. We may have our differences, but we share a common goal – defeating Lord Brevon and protecting our world.

Assistant: Well said, Spade. Let's hope their bonds, whether romantic or not, continue to strengthen their resolve in battle.

Spade: Indeed. And I'll be there, keeping them on their toes every step of the way.

[The scene ends with Spade, silently acknowledging the complexity and importance of the dynamics between Lilac, Carol, and Milla.]

[End of scene.]
Shang Tu soldiers and civilians react to When Neera Ask Lilac, Carol and Milla about Dating Naruto. Lilac, Carol and Milla: “W-What? No, we’re just… Enemie" | au: Naruto (son of brevon.). part 7

[Scene: Shang Tu soldiers and civilians gather to discuss the news of Neera's question.]

Soldier 1: Did you hear about Neera approaching Lilac, Carol, and Milla? She asked them if they ever considered dating Naruto!

Soldier 2: Really? That's unexpected. I always thought they were focused on their mission as enemies.

Civilian 1: It's interesting to know that even enemies can become subjects of romance rumors.

Civilian 2: Well, they quickly denied any such intentions, emphasizing that they see each other as enemies.

Soldier 1: That shows their commitment to their cause. Love might be a distraction they can't afford right now.

Soldier 2: Absolutely. We need them to remain dedicated and focused on protecting our city.

Civilian 1: It's impressive how they can maintain their unity despite such challenging circumstances.

Civilian 2: That's true. Love or not, their teamwork and determination are what keep us safe.

Soldier 1: Their fight against evil is what we admire and support. Personal relationships can wait.

Soldier 2: They are heroes for a reason. They know what's best for themselves and for Shang Tu.

Civilian 1: It's a relief to see that they haven't let their emotions compromise their duty.

Civilian 2: Definitely. Let's continue to rally behind them as they battle against Lord Brevon and his forces.

[The soldiers and civilians share a collective understanding that their heroes' dedication to their mission comes first, setting aside any romantic distractions.]

[End of scene.]
Shang Mu soldiers and civilians react to When Neera Ask Lilac, Carol and Milla about Dating Naruto. Lilac, Carol and Milla: “W-What? No, we’re just… Enemie" | au: Naruto (son of brevon.). part 8

[Scene: Shang Mu soldiers and civilians gather to discuss the news of Neera's question.]

Soldier 1: Did you hear about the question Neera asked Lilac, Carol, and Milla? She asked if they ever thought about dating Naruto!

Soldier 2: Really? That's quite unexpected. They're known for their bravery and loyalty.

Civilian 1: I never would have imagined such a question. They've always seemed focused on their battles against Lord Brevon.

Civilian 2: It's a testament to their dedication that they dismissed the idea without hesitation.

Soldier 1: They have a mission to protect Shang Mu and defeat their enemies. Personal entanglements can complicate matters.

Soldier 2: That's true. It's important for them to maintain their focus and not let romance distract them from their duty.

Civilian 1: They have our support either way. They're heroes fighting for our safety, and we trust their judgment.

Civilian 2: Absolutely. Their unity and determination are what make them formidable. Love or not, they are our protectors.

Soldier 1: They've proven time and again that they can overcome any obstacle. Let's continue to stand by them.

Soldier 2: Agreed. We'll keep cheering them on, knowing that their dedication to our safety never wavers.

Civilian 1: Their unwavering commitment inspires us all. We're lucky to have such brave warriors defending Shang Mu.

Civilian 2: We'll show our gratitude by supporting them in their battles, allowing them to focus on what matters most.

[The soldiers and civilians express their unwavering support for Lilac, Carol, and Milla, understanding the importance of their dedication and focusing on the greater good.]

[End of scene.]
